draft.title: weather app for runners
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
weather app for runners

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Runner weather apps benefit from simple, colorful icon systems that are legible at a glance and feel modern rather than photorealistic
facade.label: Bright, flat icons
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers clean, geometric, playful visual language; likely values quick scanning during runs
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer photorealistic imagery, detailed gradients, or more sophisticated/minimal icon systems

facade.hypothesis: Runners expect real-time data to animate in sync with physiological rhythms, not update on fixed intervals
facade.axisTargeted: Rhythm: synchronized biological vs mechanistic time
facade.label: Pace pulses with heartbeat
facade.acceptImplies: Explore time-synced animations, data tied to HR patterns, organic refresh cadences
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward predictable, clock-based updates and instant state changes

evidence.hypothesis: Runner weather apps benefit from simple, colorful icon systems that are legible at a glance and feel modern rather than photorealistic
evidence.implication: User prefers clean, geometric, playful visual language; likely values quick scanning during runs

facade.hypothesis: Runners need to decide between detailed metrics (pace, HR, elevation) shown separately vs a single unified weather readiness score
facade.axisTargeted: Granular data presentation vs reductive summary
facade.label: Splits vs overview
facade.acceptImplies: User values exploring individual weather variables to make run decisions; design should show metrics in separate cards or columns
facade.rejectImplies: User wants one quick answer (e.g., 'Good running weather: Yes/No' or a single score); design should prioritize simplicity over choice

facade.hypothesis: Runners need immediate visual clarity in bright sunlight—stark blacks and bright whites create urgency and readability on the go
facade.label: Bold, high contrast
facade.acceptImplies: User values crisp, machine-like precision and high legibility over warmth; interface should feel engineered and performance-focused
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers softer gradations, anti-glare palettes, or a more natural visual hierarchy; interface should feel approachable and less aggressive

facade.hypothesis: Runners need persistent access to key metrics (pace, distance, time) while browsing upcoming runs; a fixed sidebar with scrolling card grid balances scannability with detail.
facade.label: Sidebar + card grid
facade.acceptImplies: User values always-visible stats and modular run cards; likely wants glanceable data + flexible browsing.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers full-screen focus or tab-based separation; likely wants immersive single-view or progressive disclosure over persistent chrome.

facade.hypothesis: A conversational tone that balances approachability with credibility—using casual language and humor while maintaining authority on running/weather advice.
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and Personality — friendly vs professional
facade.label: Friendly but helpful
facade.acceptImplies: The app should feel like a knowledgeable friend giving running tips, using contractions, exclamation points, and encouraging language. Runners want personality, not just data.
facade.rejectImplies: Lean toward a more neutral, information-first voice that prioritizes clarity and professionalism. Personality takes a backseat; users come for accuracy, not charm.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Runner weather apps need high contrast between background and typography to remain readable in bright daylight conditions during outdoor runs
facade.axisTargeted: Light vs dark polarity and legibility optimization
facade.label: Dark background, bright text
facade.acceptImplies: User values practical readability over aesthetic trends; likely prefers functional dark interfaces that reduce eye strain in sunlight
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer softer contrast, gradient backgrounds, or prioritizes visual warmth/cohesion over maximum legibility during athletic activity

draft.title: RunWeather
draft.summary: A weather app designed for runners, showing real-time conditions, wind impact, and run-friendly forecasts at a glance.
draft.html:
RunWeather

San Francisco, CA

62 °F
Partly Cloudy
Wind
8 mph
Humidity
65%
Feels Like
58°F
UV Index
3

Next 6 Hours

2:00 PM Partly Cloudy 64°F
3:00 PM Sunny 66°F
4:00 PM Sunny 65°F
5:00 PM Partly Cloudy 62°F
